messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS\n\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: Express\n- styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "setup-project",
      "title": "Initialize project and install dependencies",
      "description": "Create project folder, initialize npm, and install Express, Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, autoprefixer, and a dev server build tooling.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Project folder contains package.json and node_modules with Express and Tailwind-related packages installed."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS build",
      "description": "Add Tailwind config, PostCSS config and npm scripts to build or watch Tailwind CSS output, and create base input CSS file referencing Tailwind directives.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "setup-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tailwind configuration files exist and npm build/watch script produces an output CSS file containing Tailwind utilities."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-server",
      "title": "Implement Express server to serve frontend",
      "description": "Create an Express app (e.g., server.js or app.js) with static file serving for built CSS and HTML, and a root route that serves the frontend.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "setup-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts without errors and serves index.html and the generated CSS at expected routes."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-frontend",
      "title": "Build frontend HTML with Hello World element",
      "description": "Create index.html (or template) that includes the built Tailwind CSS, has a visible \"hello world\" text element, and placeholder area for the color UI controls.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "index.html loads in browser, displays the \"hello world\" text styled by Tailwind, and reserves space for UI controls."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-color-ui",
      "title": "Add frontend UI to modify text color",
      "description": "Implement JavaScript-driven UI controls (e.g., color picker, preset buttons) that change the \"hello world\" text color by modifying classes or inline styles live.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-frontend"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "User can change the \"hello world\" text color via the frontend UI and see immediate visual updates in the page."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-api",
      "title": "Optional: Add API to save and load color preference",
      "description": "Add Express endpoints (POST/GET) to persist the selected color (in-memory or simple JSON file) and add frontend calls to save/load preferences.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-server",
        "implement-color-ui"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Frontend can save a selected color to the server and reload the saved color on page load (persistence verified)."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-document",
      "title": "Test functionality and provide run instructions",
      "description": "Manually test the app across common browsers, verify color changes and API persistence (if implemented), and write README with setup and usage steps.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-color-ui",
        "create-server",
        "configure-tailwind"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README with clear run/build instructions exists and tests confirm the UI changes colors and server serves files correctly."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down decomposition: set up project and tooling first, configure styling build, implement server to serve assets, build frontend structure, add interactive color UI, optionally add persistence API, then test and document."
}
